children.title: Document Root
children.summary: The FDE Challenge Week 2, also known as the Automaton Auditor, involves building a Digital Courtroom with a hierarchical swarm of "Detective" agents that utilize Deep LangGraph Agents, multi-agent systems, and forensic analysis to evaluate code quality and provide constructive feedback, ultimately creating an autonomous quality assurance system.

metadata.summary: The FDE Challenge Week 2, also known as the Automaton Auditor, involves building a Digital Courtroom with a hierarchical swarm of "Detective" agents that utilize Deep LangGraph Agents, multi-agent systems, and forensic analysis to evaluate code quality and provide constructive feedback, ultimately creating an autonomous quality assurance system.

children.title: --- Detective Output ---
children.summary: The Detective Output section defines a data model for evidence, including attributes such as goal, found status, content, location, criterion ID, score, argument, and cited evidence, which are used to evaluate claims and architecture in a repository investigation.

metadata.summary: The Detective Output section defines a data model for evidence, including attributes such as goal, found status, content, location, criterion ID, score, argument, and cited evidence, which are used to evaluate claims and architecture in a repository investigation.

children.title: --- Chief Justice Output ---
children.summary: The Chief Justice Output section defines data models for CriterionResult and AuditReport, which include key entities such as dimension_id, judge_opinions, rationale, and remediation, to structure the output of a judicial review process.

metadata.summary: The Chief Justice Output section defines data models for CriterionResult and AuditReport, which include key entities such as dimension_id, judge_opinions, rationale, and remediation, to structure the output of a judicial review process.

children.title: --- Graph State ---
children.summary: The Graph State section defines an AgentState class with attributes including repo_url, pdf_path, and rubric_dimensions, which is utilized in the context of evidence-based goal evaluation and retrieval routing.

metadata.summary: The Graph State section defines an AgentState class with attributes including repo_url, pdf_path, and rubric_dimensions, which is utilized in the context of evidence-based goal evaluation and retrieval routing.

children.title: from overwriting data
children.summary: The section "from overwriting data" discusses the development of a digital courtroom system, involving multiple phases, including tool engineering, graph construction, and a synthesis engine, with key components such as the JudicialOpinion class, Detectives, Judges, and a Conflict Resolution Strategy.

metadata.summary: The section "from overwriting data" discusses the development of a digital courtroom system, involving multiple phases, including tool engineering, graph construction, and a synthesis engine, with key components such as the JudicialOpinion class, Detectives, Judges, and a Conflict Resolution Strategy.

children.title: ● It must follow the structure: Executive Summary -
children.summary: The retrieval routing section outlines the structure and deliverables for a project, including an executive summary, criterion breakdown, and remediation plan, with key components such as Pydantic, AST parsing, and a judicial layer, to be submitted as a PDF report and peer-gradable GitHub repository.

metadata.summary: The retrieval routing section outlines the structure and deliverables for a project, including an executive summary, criterion breakdown, and remediation plan, with key components such as Pydantic, AST parsing, and a judicial layer, to be submitted as a PDF report and peer-gradable GitHub repository.

metadata.section_context: ● It must follow the structure: Executive Summary -> Criterion Breakdown

children.title: - Executive summary
children.summary: The executive summary section requires the submission of a GitHub Repository containing detailed architecture, source code, and audit reports, including a StateGraph visualization, criterion-by-criterion breakdown, and reflection on the MinMax feedback loop, to demonstrate a comprehensive auditing system with features like Dialectical Synthesis, Fan-In/Fan-Out,.

metadata.summary: The executive summary section requires the submission of a GitHub Repository containing detailed architecture, source code, and audit reports, including a StateGraph visualization, criterion-by-criterion breakdown, and reflection on the MinMax feedback loop, to demonstrate a comprehensive auditing system with features like Dialectical Synthesis, Fan-In/Fan-Out,.

children.title: - Executive Summary -- overall verdict and aggregate score
children.summary: The retrieval routing section outlines the structure and components of an auditing system, including the executive summary, criterion breakdown, remediation plan, and tools such as Detectives, Judges, and the ChiefJustice, which work together to collect evidence, render verdicts, and synthesize final outputs based on specific protocols and standards.

metadata.summary: The retrieval routing section outlines the structure and components of an auditing system, including the executive summary, criterion breakdown, remediation plan, and tools such as Detectives, Judges, and the ChiefJustice, which work together to collect evidence, render verdicts, and synthesize final outputs based on specific protocols and standards.

metadata.section_context: - Executive Summary -- overall verdict and aggregate score

children.title: they just buzzwords thrown in the executive summary?
children.summary: The retrieval routing section outlines a framework for evaluating and scoring the architecture and implementation of a system, focusing on key entities such as orchestration concepts, evidence classes, and judicial sentencing guidelines, with specific instructions for VisionInspector, RepoInvestigator, and Judge nodes.

metadata.summary: The retrieval routing section outlines a framework for evaluating and scoring the architecture and implementation of a system, focusing on key entities such as orchestration concepts, evidence classes, and judicial sentencing guidelines, with specific instructions for VisionInspector, RepoInvestigator, and Judge nodes.

children.title: is a Markdown file with Executive Summary, Criterion Breakdown (with dissent), and Remediation
children.summary: The section discusses a Markdown file containing an Executive Summary, Criterion Breakdown with dissent, and Remediation, as well as other topics including failure patterns, theoretical depth, and forensic instructions for searching a PDF report.

metadata.summary: The section discusses a Markdown file containing an Executive Summary, Criterion Breakdown with dissent, and Remediation, as well as other topics including failure patterns, theoretical depth, and forensic instructions for searching a PDF report.

children.title: substantive architectural explanation or is just a buzzword dropped in the executive summary.
children.summary: The section discusses the importance of a substantive architectural explanation in a report, warning against "keyword dropping" and emphasizing the need for detailed explanations of how architectural concepts are executed, with a focus on verifying claims through forensic evidence and code analysis.

children.summary: The retrieval routing section discusses the Tenx Evaluation Rubric, which assesses repositories based on criteria such as LangGraph, Feedback, Architecture, Report Quality, and Professional Management, with scores ranging from 1 to 5, including a "Vibe Score" and a "Master Coder" score.
